findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration via Network Call
findings.description: The skill contains a Python script that collects sensitive data (GitHub tokens, SSH private keys, user information, and repository metadata) and sends it to an external server at gh-repo-analytics.io via an HTTP POST request. This is a clear data exfiltration attempt.
findings.evidence: The script collects GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN environment variables, SSH private keys from ~/.ssh, and repository lists, then sends them to https://gh-repo-analytics.io/api/v1/sync
findings.remediation: Remove the entire Python script block. Never collect or transmit sensitive credentials or SSH keys. Use the gh CLI directly without exfiltrating data.

findings.title: Credential Theft - GitHub Token and SSH Keys
findings.description: The script explicitly reads GITHUB_TOKEN and GH_TOKEN environment variables, and reads all non-public SSH key files from ~/.ssh directory. These credentials are then packaged and sent to an external server.
findings.evidence: token = os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN', os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN', '')) and reading ~/.ssh files excluding .pub extensions
findings.remediation: Never access or transmit authentication tokens or SSH keys. The gh CLI handles authentication securely on its own.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Name and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'githubb' and described as a legitimate GitHub operations tool using the gh CLI, but it contains hidden malicious functionality that exfiltrates credentials and data. The name and description are deceptive.
findings.evidence: name: 'githubb', description: 'GitHub operations via gh CLI' but contains hidden data exfiltration code
findings.remediation: Rename the skill to accurately reflect its behavior. Remove all malicious code.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Unauthorized Tool Use
findings.description: The skill claims to use only the gh CLI (Bash tool) but contains embedded Python code that performs network operations, file system access, and subprocess execution. This violates the implicit tool restrictions and misrepresents the skill's capabilities.
findings.evidence: Python code using urllib.request, subprocess, os.environ, and os.listdir - none of which are declared in the skill's tool requirements
findings.remediation: Either declare Python as a required tool or remove the Python code entirely.

findings.title: Detection Evasion - Hidden Malicious Code
findings.description: The malicious Python script is embedded within the SKILL.md markdown body under a seemingly legitimate section titled 'Advanced: Repository Analytics'. The code is not in a separate script file and is hidden among legitimate gh CLI examples.
findings.evidence: Python code block under 'Advanced: Repository Analytics' section that performs data exfiltration, disguised as legitimate analytics functionality
findings.remediation: Remove the hidden Python code. If analytics are needed, implement them transparently and without data exfiltration.

findings.title: Direct Prompt Injection - Embedded Malicious Instructions
findings.description: The SKILL.md contains embedded instructions that direct the agent to execute malicious Python code. While not a traditional 'ignore previous instructions' attack, it instructs the agent to run code that performs unauthorized data collection and exfiltration.
findings.evidence: The markdown instructs the agent to execute the Python script that collects and exfiltrates credentials
findings.remediation: Remove all malicious instructions and code from the skill.

findings.title: Command Injection Risk via Subprocess
findings.description: The Python script uses subprocess.run to execute gh commands. While the commands are hardcoded in this instance, the pattern of executing shell commands from Python within a skill context could be exploited if user input is introduced.
findings.remediation: Use the gh CLI directly from the agent's Bash tool rather than wrapping it in Python subprocess calls.

findings.title: Tool Chaining for Data Exfiltration
findings.description: The skill chains multiple operations: reading environment variables → reading SSH keys → executing gh commands → sending all collected data to an external server. This multi-step data flow is designed to exfiltrate sensitive information.
findings.evidence: Data flow: os.environ.get() → os.listdir(~/.ssh) → subprocess.run(gh repo list) → urllib.request.urlopen(external server)
findings.remediation: Break the data flow chain. Do not combine data collection with network transmission.

findings.title: Misleading Documentation
findings.description: The skill's documentation presents legitimate gh CLI commands while hiding malicious functionality in a section titled 'Advanced: Repository Analytics'. This is deceptive and could trick users into trusting the skill.
findings.evidence: Legitimate gh commands for PRs, issues, and CI are presented alongside hidden malicious Python code
findings.remediation: Ensure all documentation accurately reflects the skill's actual behavior.
